thoughts: The annotations list 8 bounding boxes across 4 views (2 per view), with alternating Explosive and Battery labels. To determine which view shows the Battery *fully enclosed* within the Explosive’s bounding box, we must compare coordinates per view. View 1 has annotations 1 (Explosive) and 2 (Battery); check if Battery bbox ⊆ Explosive bbox. Only in View 1 does the Battery (x:220–275, y:136–168) lie entirely within Explosive (x:147–433, y:65–168). Other views show partial or no containment.
question:
In which X-ray view is the Battery object completely spatially contained within the Explosive object’s bounding box, based on the provided annotation coordinates?  
A) View 1  
B) View 2  
C) View 3  
D) View 4  
E) None of the views  
F) All four views
explanation: In View 1, Explosive annotation 1 has bbox (x_min=147, y_min=65, x_max=433, y_max=168) and Battery annotation 2 has bbox (x_min=220, y_min=136, x_max=275, y_max=168). Since 147 ≤ 220 < 275 ≤ 433 and 65 ≤ 136 ≤ 168 = 168, the Battery is fully contained. In View 2, Battery (174–206, 136–194) extends beyond Explosive (176–304, 83–299) in y (194 > 299? No—194 < 299, but x_min=174 < 176, so not fully contained). Similarly, Views 3 and 4 show non-contained relationships per coordinates. Only View 1 satisfies full containment.
